The major program includes research and internship experiences as well as field experience to help prepare students for these career paths.</p>","groupId":"5acc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p><strong style=\"color: rgb(78, 14, 80);\">Group 2, Group 3, Group 4 and Group 5: </strong>Please note that students may be able to meet major requirements with different combinations of courses. Please visit the major for more details and advising.</p>","groupId":"5ccc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":9,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>At the time of <a href=\"https://www.ucdavis.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer/transfer-admission-guarantee\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">TAG</a> submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum <strong>3.20 </strong>cumulative GPA and at least a <strong>2.50</strong> GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.</p>","groupId":"5fcc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>This is a competitive major and is subject to the <a href=\"https://www.ucdavis.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer/selective-major-requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Selective Major Review</a> process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.</p><p> </p><p>Important Information:</p><p><br></p><p>You must complete the courses below and maintain a<strong> 2.50 </strong>GPA in each series:</p><p>●     Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C</p><p>●     Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C</p><p>●     Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B</p><p><br></p><p>Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission)</p><p><br></p><p>●     Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C</p><p>●     Physics 7A/B/C</p><p><br></p><p>Visit Department agreements as needed</p><p><br></p><p>Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (<a href=\"https://ua.ucdavis.edu/portal/find-my-advisor\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Find my advisor</a>)</p><p><br></p><p>Note:</p><p><strong>Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:</strong></p><p>AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">General Catalog</a>. Quick reference charts for <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/APCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">AP</a> and <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/IBCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">IB</a>.</p>","groupId":"74cc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>The major in <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Marine & Coastal Science </a>focuses on the interdisciplinary nature of marine sciences by exposing students to core, breadth, and focus area courses in the discipline, in addition to a strong foundation of science preparatory material. The major builds upon UC Davis strengths in marine and coastal sciences, including field-based courses offered at Bodega Marine Laboratory to provide students a unique, interdisciplinary, “hands on” education. Advising is provided by the Department of Earth & Planetary Sciences for interested students.</p><p><br></p><p>The major begins with introductory courses in mathematics, chemistry, physics, biology, and earth sciences. These are followed by core courses in Marine Science. The major requirements provide focus and breadth, so that each student gains mastery in one area and broad exposure to many facets of Marine & Coastal Science. Focus areas are: (i) Coastal Environmental Processes, (ii) Marine Ecology & Organismal Biology, (iii) Marine Environmental Chemistry, and (iv) Oceans & the Earth System.</p><p><br></p><p>In this major, students will be exposed to the foundation disciplines within marine science (biology, chemistry, geology, physics) as well as modern issues facing marine and coastal environments; e.g., climate change, pollution, carbon cycling, and conservation. The major requires field experience, independent research or internship, and concludes with a capstone course featuring current research in marine science. These integrative experiences will require students to synthesize the interdisciplinary topics that they have encountered through this degree program. The mastery achieved provides a strong foundation for future careers in academic science, government, policy, and the private sector.</p>","groupId":"75cc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"}]
